title: Garden of Eden
wikitext:
[]: Garden of Eden?             Quote Post by AWinters » Wed Dec 21, 2022 7:41 pm In the 21st episode, the Geofront is revealed to Fuyutsuki as something they uncovered that was preserved that way. Could it be (figuratively or literally) the Garden of Eden? Maybe the black moon was the garden that humans were banished from so long ago.

[]: Re: Garden of Eden?             Quote Post by Blockio » Wed Dec 21, 2022 10:12 pm This is one of those cases where I feel like there's a divide between out of universe themes and in-universe lore; out of universe (if my admittedly somewhat shaky memory of ep 21 is not failing me), it makes sense for the Black Moon to be figurative for the Garden of Eden in some capacity, but in-universe I think that would be pushing the interpetation a bit far

[]: Re: Garden of Eden?             Quote Post by AWinters » Thu Dec 22, 2022 10:42 am Are there any previously discussed explanations for how perfectly landscaped the Geofront was? It seems to be presented as a long forgotten paradise.

[]: Re: Garden of Eden?             Quote Post by Blockio » Thu Dec 22, 2022 3:52 pm Geofront is stated pretty early on to be manmade

[]: Re: Garden of Eden?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Dec 22, 2022 6:22 pm The early introduction is "this is our secret base"; the reveal comes a lot later Episode 21 wrote: FUYUTSUKI:“What is this?” IKARI (OFF):“A cavern left behind by someone, who was not us. Though eighty-nine percent of it is buried.”

[]: Re: Garden of Eden?             Quote Post by gelflinghand » Fri Dec 23, 2022 3:03 pm The landscaping would be recent, but if Lilith has been there the whole time, then yes, the Geofront is where the first humans were created, and they could well remember it as a paradise after they were sent out to populate the rest of the planet.

[]: Re: Garden of Eden?             Quote Post by AWinters » Fri Dec 23, 2022 5:12 pm The further thought occurs that they set up base at ground zero of mankind and called it "Project Eden". It seems to be recognised as a figurative or literal Eden in universe. The landscaping and building being their own work makes sense though. It should have occurred to me that if they'd had time to start the buildings they would have had time for gardening too.

[]: Re: Garden of Eden?             Quote Post by 2Lacissal2 » Tue Dec 27, 2022 7:04 am I wouldn’t say humans were created in the Geofront. The themes of evolution running through Eva and the section on Lilith in the Classified Information implies there’s a sort of pre-programmed evolution going on, that Lilith releasing the primordial soup of LCL will inevitably develop into Lilin to receive FAR souls. I don’t think vegetation would grow naturally in the Geofront without the light reflectors/condensers they’ve constructed.

[]: Re: Garden of Eden?             Quote Post by sithsauron » Wed Dec 28, 2022 10:44 pm Perhaps the garden of eden was the south pole where Adam was found rendered inert by the Lance of longinus after first impact and got frozen over due to climate change

[]: Re: Garden of Eden?             Quote Post by jimmyanderson2023 » Fri Mar 03, 2023 3:44 pm It is a pleasure to meet all of you. I am new here. The reason I'm here is because I'm watching Garden of Eden. I love it.

[]: Re: Garden of Eden?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Mar 11, 2023 9:49 am That line in ep 21 is the closest the show comes to explicitly confirming/mentioning the precursors/FAR.

[]: Re: Garden of Eden?             Quote Post by Asugran233 » Thu Jun 22, 2023 12:46 pm Blockio wrote: This is one of those cases where I feel like there's a divide between out of universe themes and in-universe lore; out of universe (if my admittedly somewhat shaky memory of ep 21 is not failing me), it makes sense for the Black Moon to be figurative for the Garden of Eden in some capacity, but in-universe I think that would be pushing the interpetation a bit far

[]: Re: Garden of Eden?             Quote Post by Asunji_Yuko » Fri Jun 23, 2023 1:04 pm AWinters wrote: In the 21st episode, the Geofront is revealed to Fuyutsuki as something they uncovered that was preserved that way. Could it be (figuratively or literally) the Garden of Eden? Maybe the black moon was the garden that humans were banished from so long ago.
